SEARCH
Package com.cellsimulation.model

Class SimulationEngine

java.lang.Object
com.cellsimulation.model.SimulationEngine
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class SimulationEngine extends Object implements Serializable
Orchestrator of the disease-propagation simulation.

The engine owns the Grid (the playground), the SimulationSettings (the probabilities and durations) and the current NeighborhoodStrategy. It is intentionally passive: it knows how to play exactly one tick when step() is called, but it never spawns a thread or schedules a timer. The simulation cadence is driven from the outside, by the JavaFX window or by the CLI runner.

A single tick proceeds as follows:

  1. increment tickCount;
  2. snapshot every Person currently on the grid;
  3. for each snapshotted person, call update then move then spread;
  4. notify every SimulationListener via onTick.

The snapshot is critical: without it, a person that has just moved to a yet-unvisited cell would be processed twice in the same tick.

The engine is Serializable so that the whole simulation state can be persisted to disk through the SaveService. The listeners field is transient because listeners (such as a UI controller) are not necessarily serializable; after deserialization the engine starts with an empty listener list and callers are expected to re-attach their listeners.

See Also:

  • Constructor Details

    • SimulationEngine

      public SimulationEngine(Grid grid, SimulationSettings settings, NeighborhoodStrategy neighborhood)
      Creates a new simulation engine wired to the given grid, settings and neighborhood strategy.

      The engine starts paused (running == false) with a tick count of zero and no registered listener.

      Parameters:
      grid - the playground; must not be null
      settings - the simulation settings; must not be null
      neighborhood - the initial neighborhood strategy; must not be null
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if any argument is null

  • Method Details

    • step

      public void step()
      Plays a single simulation tick.

      The method first increments the tick counter, then snapshots every person currently on the grid. Each snapshotted person is processed in the strict order update -> move -> spread; deceased persons are no-ops because their three methods short-circuit on the deceased state. Finally every listener is notified through onTick; the notification iterates over a defensive copy of the listener list, so that a listener that unsubscribes itself during onTick does not break the iteration.

      step() does not modify the running flag: the engine can be advanced one tick at a time even when paused.

    • start

      public void start()
      Switches the engine to its "running" state.

      No thread is started: the engine remains passive. The caller is responsible for repeatedly invoking step() at the desired cadence as long as isRunning() returns true.

    • pause

      public void pause()
      Switches the engine to its "paused" state.

      The tick counter is preserved; only the running flag flips to false.

    • reset

      public void reset()
      Resets the simulation to an empty state.

      Concretely the method pauses the engine, resets the tick counter to zero, empties every cell of the grid (the grid dimensions and toric mode are preserved) and finally notifies every listener through SimulationListener.onReset(). The notification iterates over a defensive copy of the listener list.

      The current SimulationSettings and NeighborhoodStrategy are kept; only the inhabitants are removed.

    • addCell

      public void addCell(Position position, DiseaseState state)
      Creates a new Person with the given initial state and places it on the grid at the specified position.

      In bounded mode an out-of-bounds position causes Grid.setCell(Position, Person) to throw; the exception is propagated unchanged.

      Parameters:
      position - the target position; must not be null
      state - the initial health state of the new person; must not be null
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if position or state is null, or (in bounded mode) if position lies outside the grid

    • removeCell

      public void removeCell(Position position)
      Removes the inhabitant of the given cell, if any.

      Delegates to Grid.removeCell(Position). Provided on the engine so that the UI layer can manipulate the simulation through a single API.

      Parameters:
      position - the position to clear; must not be null
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if position is null, or (in bounded mode) if it lies outside the grid

    • addListener

      public void addListener(SimulationListener listener)
      Registers a listener that will be notified at every tick and on every reset.
      Parameters:
      listener - the listener to register; must not be null
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if listener is null

    • removeListener

      public void removeListener(SimulationListener listener)
      Unregisters a previously registered listener.

      The operation is idempotent: removing a listener that is not in the list has no effect and does not throw.

      Parameters:
      listener - the listener to unregister

    • setNeighborhood

      public void setNeighborhood(NeighborhoodStrategy neighborhood)
      Replaces the current neighborhood strategy with a new one.

      This is intended to be called by the UI when the user switches the neighborhood mode from the selector. The change takes effect on the very next tick.

      Parameters:
      neighborhood - the new strategy; must not be null
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if neighborhood is null

    • getGrid

      public Grid getGrid()
      Returns:
      the grid owned by this engine

    • getSettings

      public SimulationSettings getSettings()
      Returns:
      the simulation settings owned by this engine

    • getNeighborhood

      public NeighborhoodStrategy getNeighborhood()
      Returns:
      the neighborhood strategy currently in use

    • isRunning

      public boolean isRunning()
      Returns:
      true if the engine is in its "running" state, false if it is paused

    • getTickCount

      public int getTickCount()
      Returns:
      the number of ticks played since the last reset
